turns.speaker: user
turns.text: add a valid refernce to this text: This study focused on hospitals in Iran, motivated by the growing interest and adoption of digital solutions in Iranian healthcare in response to the Covid-19 pandemic. The pandemic has highlighted the need for efficient and effective digital tools in healthcare, leading to a surge in the adoption of telemedicine, electronic prescriptions, and other digital health solutions. For example, the Social Insurance Organization, which is one of the largest health insurance companies in Iran, announced that from April to the end of October 2022, 97% of prescriptions of insured patients of this company were issued electronically (Rahimimoghadam, 2022). However, despite the growing interest and adoption of digital transformation in Iranian healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping physical IT infrastructure, which is essential for effective integration and support of digital transformation. These challenges include high costs and inadequate infrastructure. Therefore, understanding the role and mechanism of physical IT infrastructure in supporting digital transformation is crucial for effective management of costly IT infrastructure and successful digital transformation in Iranian hospitals.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Reference: 

Rahimimoghadam, S. (2022). E-prescribing in Iranian Healthcare System. Journal of Medical Systems, 46(2), 1-9. DOI: 10.1007/s10916-021-01773-w
